---
name: nugetfetch
description: "Lightweight, AOT-friendly NuGet client library for .NET: query versions, download and extract .nupkg packages, and search nuget.org. It is NOT the official NuGet.Protocol / NuGet.Client API — the surface is much smaller and the shapes differ. Key rules: you bring your own HttpClient (NuGetClient has no parameterless ctor), extraction lives on a separate static PackageExtractor class, and NormalizeVersion is static. See the body."

<!-- GENERATED from AGENTS.md by `grounding sync-skill`. Do not edit. -->

# NuGetFetch

[![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/NuGetFetch)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGetFetch)

A lightweight NuGet client library for downloading and extracting
NuGet packages. Designed for AOT compatibility with zero
HttpClient ownership — bring your own.

```shell
dotnet add package NuGetFetch
```

## Features

- **Stream-based** — All JSON from streams, no string buffering
- **AOT compatible** — STJ source generation, no reflection
- **No HttpClient** — Accepts `HttpClient` from the caller
- **Two-tier caching** — Reads `~/.nuget/packages`, writes app cache
- **Source resolution** — Parses `nuget.config` files (with credentials)
- **TFM resolution** — Selects highest-priority target framework
- **Version resolution** — Full ascending list, latest, wildcard patterns, prerelease
- **Search** — Query nuget.org with prefix filtering

## Usage

```csharp
using NuGetFetch;

HttpClient httpClient = new();
NuGetClient client = new(httpClient);

// Get latest version
string? version = await client.GetLatestVersionAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");

// List every published version, ascending (oldest first, newest last)
IReadOnlyList<string> versions = await client.GetVersionsAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");
string oldest = versions[0];
string newest = versions[^1];

// Download and extract
using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", version!);
string extractPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "Newtonsoft.Json");
await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, extractPath);

// Find the best TFM
string? tfmPath = TfmResolver.ResolvePackagePath(extractPath);
```

### Search

```csharp
SearchService search = new(httpClient);

// Search for packages
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchAsync("json serializer");

// Prefix search
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchByPrefixAsync("Newtonsoft");
```

### Caching

Two-tier cache: reads from `~/.nuget/packages` and writes
to an app-specific cache.

```csharp
PackageCache cache = new("my-app");

// Check cache first
string? cached = cache.TryGet("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");

if (cached is null)
{
    // Download, extract, then cache
    using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");
    string tempPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), Guid.NewGuid().ToString());
    await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, tempPath);
    cached = cache.Cache("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3", tempPath);
}
```

### Source Resolution

```csharp
// Auto-discover from nuget.config files
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources();

// Or specify explicitly
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources(
    explicitSource: "https://my-feed.example.com/v3/index.json");
```

### Version Patterns

```csharp
// Resolve wildcard patterns
string? version = await client.ResolveVersionPatternAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.*");

// Parse "Package@Version" specs
PackageIdentity? parsed = PackageExtractor.ParsePackageReference("Newtonsoft.Json@13.0.3");
```

### Tool wrapper packages

.NET tools published as runtime-specific (e.g. NativeAOT) executables ship a thin wrapper
package whose only payload is a `DotnetToolSettings.xml` manifest pointing at per-RID packages
(`<id>.win-x64`, `<id>.osx-arm64`, `<id>.any`, …). To read managed metadata, redirect to the
portable `any` payload:

```csharp
string extractPath = PackageExtractor.Extract(nupkgPath, destination);

// Returns "mytool.any" when the package is a wrapper with no managed libraries; else null.
string? payloadId = PackageExtractor.TryGetToolWrapperRedirect(extractPath);
if (payloadId is not null)
{
    // Fetch + extract payloadId at the same version, then inspect that.
}
```

## API Overview

| Class              | Kind     | Purpose                      |
| ------------------ | -------- | ---------------------------- |
| `NuGetClient`      | Instance | Versions, download           |
| `SearchService`    | Instance | Search and prefix search     |
| `PackageCache`     | Instance | Two-tier package cache       |
| `ResponseCache`    | Instance | Disk cache with TTL          |
| `PackageExtractor` | Static   | Extract `.nupkg`, parse      |
| `SourceResolver`   | Static   | Parse `nuget.config` files   |
| `TfmResolver`      | Static   | Select best TFM              |
| `NuGetApi`         | Static   | Stream JSON deserialization  |

## Design

Follows the [distroessed][1] library patterns:

[1]: https://github.com/richlander/distroessed

- **POCOs** — Records with primary constructors for all data models
- **STJ source generation** — `NuGetJsonContext` for AOT-safe JSON
- **Stream-based helpers** — `NuGetApi` for stream deserialization
- **No HttpClient ownership** — Library never creates an `HttpClient`

## Dependencies

- [`NuGet.Versioning`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGet.Versioning)
  — Version parsing and comparison

</skill-context>

<!-- GENERATED from AGENTS.md by `grounding sync-skill`. Do not edit. -->

# NuGetFetch

[![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/NuGetFetch)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGetFetch)

A lightweight NuGet client library for downloading and extracting
NuGet packages. Designed for AOT compatibility with zero
HttpClient ownership — bring your own.

```shell
dotnet add package NuGetFetch
```

## Features

- **Stream-based** — All JSON from streams, no string buffering
- **AOT compatible** — STJ source generation, no reflection
- **No HttpClient** — Accepts `HttpClient` from the caller
- **Two-tier caching** — Reads `~/.nuget/packages`, writes app cache
- **Source resolution** — Parses `nuget.config` files (with credentials)
- **TFM resolution** — Selects highest-priority target framework
- **Version resolution** — Full ascending list, latest, wildcard patterns, prerelease
- **Search** — Query nuget.org with prefix filtering

## Usage

```csharp
using NuGetFetch;

HttpClient httpClient = new();
NuGetClient client = new(httpClient);

// Get latest version
string? version = await client.GetLatestVersionAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");

// List every published version, ascending (oldest first, newest last)
IReadOnlyList<string> versions = await client.GetVersionsAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");
string oldest = versions[0];
string newest = versions[^1];

// Download and extract
using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", version!);
string extractPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "Newtonsoft.Json");
await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, extractPath);

// Find the best TFM
string? tfmPath = TfmResolver.ResolvePackagePath(extractPath);
```

### Search

```csharp
SearchService search = new(httpClient);

// Search for packages
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchAsync("json serializer");

// Prefix search
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchByPrefixAsync("Newtonsoft");
```

### Caching

Two-tier cache: reads from `~/.nuget/packages` and writes
to an app-specific cache.

```csharp
PackageCache cache = new("my-app");

// Check cache first
string? cached = cache.TryGet("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");

if (cached is null)
{
    // Download, extract, then cache
    using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");
    string tempPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), Guid.NewGuid().ToString());
    await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, tempPath);
    cached = cache.Cache("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3", tempPath);
}
```

### Source Resolution

```csharp
// Auto-discover from nuget.config files
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources();

// Or specify explicitly
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources(
    explicitSource: "https://my-feed.example.com/v3/index.json");
```

### Version Patterns

```csharp
// Resolve wildcard patterns
string? version = await client.ResolveVersionPatternAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.*");

// Parse "Package@Version" specs
PackageIdentity? parsed = PackageExtractor.ParsePackageReference("Newtonsoft.Json@13.0.3");
```

### Tool wrapper packages

.NET tools published as runtime-specific (e.g. NativeAOT) executables ship a thin wrapper
package whose only payload is a `DotnetToolSettings.xml` manifest pointing at per-RID packages
(`<id>.win-x64`, `<id>.osx-arm64`, `<id>.any`, …). To read managed metadata, redirect to the
portable `any` payload:

```csharp
string extractPath = PackageExtractor.Extract(nupkgPath, destination);

// Returns "mytool.any" when the package is a wrapper with no managed libraries; else null.
string? payloadId = PackageExtractor.TryGetToolWrapperRedirect(extractPath);
if (payloadId is not null)
{
    // Fetch + extract payloadId at the same version, then inspect that.
}
```

## API Overview

| Class              | Kind     | Purpose                      |
| ------------------ | -------- | ---------------------------- |
| `NuGetClient`      | Instance | Versions, download           |
| `SearchService`    | Instance | Search and prefix search     |
| `PackageCache`     | Instance | Two-tier package cache       |
| `ResponseCache`    | Instance | Disk cache with TTL          |
| `PackageExtractor` | Static   | Extract `.nupkg`, parse      |
| `SourceResolver`   | Static   | Parse `nuget.config` files   |
| `TfmResolver`      | Static   | Select best TFM              |
| `NuGetApi`         | Static   | Stream JSON deserialization  |

## Design

Follows the [distroessed][1] library patterns:

[1]: https://github.com/richlander/distroessed

- **POCOs** — Records with primary constructors for all data models
- **STJ source generation** — `NuGetJsonContext` for AOT-safe JSON
- **Stream-based helpers** — `NuGetApi` for stream deserialization
- **No HttpClient ownership** — Library never creates an `HttpClient`

## Dependencies

- [`NuGet.Versioning`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGet.Versioning)
  — Version parsing and comparison

<!-- GENERATED from AGENTS.md by `grounding sync-skill`. Do not edit. -->

# NuGetFetch

[![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/NuGetFetch)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGetFetch)

A lightweight NuGet client library for downloading and extracting
NuGet packages. Designed for AOT compatibility with zero
HttpClient ownership — bring your own.

```shell
dotnet add package NuGetFetch
```

## Features

- **Stream-based** — All JSON from streams, no string buffering
- **AOT compatible** — STJ source generation, no reflection
- **No HttpClient** — Accepts `HttpClient` from the caller
- **Two-tier caching** — Reads `~/.nuget/packages`, writes app cache
- **Source resolution** — Parses `nuget.config` files (with credentials)
- **TFM resolution** — Selects highest-priority target framework
- **Version resolution** — Full ascending list, latest, wildcard patterns, prerelease
- **Search** — Query nuget.org with prefix filtering

## Usage

```csharp
using NuGetFetch;

HttpClient httpClient = new();
NuGetClient client = new(httpClient);

// Get latest version
string? version = await client.GetLatestVersionAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");

// List every published version, ascending (oldest first, newest last)
IReadOnlyList<string> versions = await client.GetVersionsAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");
string oldest = versions[0];
string newest = versions[^1];

// Download and extract
using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", version!);
string extractPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "Newtonsoft.Json");
await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, extractPath);

// Find the best TFM
string? tfmPath = TfmResolver.ResolvePackagePath(extractPath);
```

### Search

```csharp
SearchService search = new(httpClient);

// Search for packages
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchAsync("json serializer");

// Prefix search
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchByPrefixAsync("Newtonsoft");
```

### Caching

Two-tier cache: reads from `~/.nuget/packages` and writes
to an app-specific cache.

```csharp
PackageCache cache = new("my-app");

// Check cache first
string? cached = cache.TryGet("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");

if (cached is null)
{
    // Download, extract, then cache
    using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");
    string tempPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), Guid.NewGuid().ToString());
    await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, tempPath);
    cached = cache.Cache("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3", tempPath);
}
```

### Source Resolution

```csharp
// Auto-discover from nuget.config files
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources();

// Or specify explicitly
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources(
    explicitSource: "https://my-feed.example.com/v3/index.json");
```

### Version Patterns

```csharp
// Resolve wildcard patterns
string? version = await client.ResolveVersionPatternAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.*");

// Parse "Package@Version" specs
PackageIdentity? parsed = PackageExtractor.ParsePackageReference("Newtonsoft.Json@13.0.3");
```

### Tool wrapper packages

.NET tools published as runtime-specific (e.g. NativeAOT) executables ship a thin wrapper
package whose only payload is a `DotnetToolSettings.xml` manifest pointing at per-RID packages
(`<id>.win-x64`, `<id>.osx-arm64`, `<id>.any`, …). To read managed metadata, redirect to the
portable `any` payload:

```csharp
string extractPath = PackageExtractor.Extract(nupkgPath, destination);

// Returns "mytool.any" when the package is a wrapper with no managed libraries; else null.
string? payloadId = PackageExtractor.TryGetToolWrapperRedirect(extractPath);
if (payloadId is not null)
{
    // Fetch + extract payloadId at the same version, then inspect that.
}
```

## API Overview

| Class              | Kind     | Purpose                      |
| ------------------ | -------- | ---------------------------- |
| `NuGetClient`      | Instance | Versions, download           |
| `SearchService`    | Instance | Search and prefix search     |
| `PackageCache`     | Instance | Two-tier package cache       |
| `ResponseCache`    | Instance | Disk cache with TTL          |
| `PackageExtractor` | Static   | Extract `.nupkg`, parse      |
| `SourceResolver`   | Static   | Parse `nuget.config` files   |
| `TfmResolver`      | Static   | Select best TFM              |
| `NuGetApi`         | Static   | Stream JSON deserialization  |

## Design

Follows the [distroessed][1] library patterns:

[1]: https://github.com/richlander/distroessed

- **POCOs** — Records with primary constructors for all data models
- **STJ source generation** — `NuGetJsonContext` for AOT-safe JSON
- **Stream-based helpers** — `NuGetApi` for stream deserialization
- **No HttpClient ownership** — Library never creates an `HttpClient`

## Dependencies

- [`NuGet.Versioning`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGet.Versioning)
  — Version parsing and comparison

---
name: nugetfetch
description: "Lightweight, AOT-friendly NuGet client library for .NET: query versions, download and extract .nupkg packages, and search nuget.org. It is NOT the official NuGet.Protocol / NuGet.Client API — the surface is much smaller and the shapes differ. Key rules: you bring your own HttpClient (NuGetClient has no parameterless ctor), extraction lives on a separate static PackageExtractor class, and NormalizeVersion is static. See the body."

<!-- GENERATED from AGENTS.md by `grounding sync-skill`. Do not edit. -->

# NuGetFetch

[![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/NuGetFetch)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGetFetch)

A lightweight NuGet client library for downloading and extracting
NuGet packages. Designed for AOT compatibility with zero
HttpClient ownership — bring your own.

```shell
dotnet add package NuGetFetch
```

## Features

- **Stream-based** — All JSON from streams, no string buffering
- **AOT compatible** — STJ source generation, no reflection
- **No HttpClient** — Accepts `HttpClient` from the caller
- **Two-tier caching** — Reads `~/.nuget/packages`, writes app cache
- **Source resolution** — Parses `nuget.config` files (with credentials)
- **TFM resolution** — Selects highest-priority target framework
- **Version resolution** — Full ascending list, latest, wildcard patterns, prerelease
- **Search** — Query nuget.org with prefix filtering

## Usage

```csharp
using NuGetFetch;

HttpClient httpClient = new();
NuGetClient client = new(httpClient);

// Get latest version
string? version = await client.GetLatestVersionAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");

// List every published version, ascending (oldest first, newest last)
IReadOnlyList<string> versions = await client.GetVersionsAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");
string oldest = versions[0];
string newest = versions[^1];

// Download and extract
using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", version!);
string extractPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "Newtonsoft.Json");
await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, extractPath);

// Find the best TFM
string? tfmPath = TfmResolver.ResolvePackagePath(extractPath);
```

### Search

```csharp
SearchService search = new(httpClient);

// Search for packages
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchAsync("json serializer");

// Prefix search
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchByPrefixAsync("Newtonsoft");
```

### Caching

Two-tier cache: reads from `~/.nuget/packages` and writes
to an app-specific cache.

```csharp
PackageCache cache = new("my-app");

// Check cache first
string? cached = cache.TryGet("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");

if (cached is null)
{
    // Download, extract, then cache
    using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");
    string tempPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), Guid.NewGuid().ToString());
    await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, tempPath);
    cached = cache.Cache("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3", tempPath);
}
```

### Source Resolution

```csharp
// Auto-discover from nuget.config files
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources();

// Or specify explicitly
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources(
    explicitSource: "https://my-feed.example.com/v3/index.json");
```

### Version Patterns

```csharp
// Resolve wildcard patterns
string? version = await client.ResolveVersionPatternAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.*");

// Parse "Package@Version" specs
PackageIdentity? parsed = PackageExtractor.ParsePackageReference("Newtonsoft.Json@13.0.3");
```

### Tool wrapper packages

.NET tools published as runtime-specific (e.g. NativeAOT) executables ship a thin wrapper
package whose only payload is a `DotnetToolSettings.xml` manifest pointing at per-RID packages
(`<id>.win-x64`, `<id>.osx-arm64`, `<id>.any`, …). To read managed metadata, redirect to the
portable `any` payload:

```csharp
string extractPath = PackageExtractor.Extract(nupkgPath, destination);

// Returns "mytool.any" when the package is a wrapper with no managed libraries; else null.
string? payloadId = PackageExtractor.TryGetToolWrapperRedirect(extractPath);
if (payloadId is not null)
{
    // Fetch + extract payloadId at the same version, then inspect that.
}
```

## API Overview

| Class              | Kind     | Purpose                      |
| ------------------ | -------- | ---------------------------- |
| `NuGetClient`      | Instance | Versions, download           |
| `SearchService`    | Instance | Search and prefix search     |
| `PackageCache`     | Instance | Two-tier package cache       |
| `ResponseCache`    | Instance | Disk cache with TTL          |
| `PackageExtractor` | Static   | Extract `.nupkg`, parse      |
| `SourceResolver`   | Static   | Parse `nuget.config` files   |
| `TfmResolver`      | Static   | Select best TFM              |
| `NuGetApi`         | Static   | Stream JSON deserialization  |

## Design

Follows the [distroessed][1] library patterns:

[1]: https://github.com/richlander/distroessed

- **POCOs** — Records with primary constructors for all data models
- **STJ source generation** — `NuGetJsonContext` for AOT-safe JSON
- **Stream-based helpers** — `NuGetApi` for stream deserialization
- **No HttpClient ownership** — Library never creates an `HttpClient`

## Dependencies

- [`NuGet.Versioning`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGet.Versioning)
  — Version parsing and comparison

- **TFM resolution** — Selects highest-priority target framework
- **Version resolution** — Full ascending list, latest, wildcard patterns, prerelease
- **Search** — Query nuget.org with prefix filtering

## Usage

```csharp
using NuGetFetch;

HttpClient httpClient = new();
NuGetClient client = new(httpClient);

// Get latest version
string? version = await client.GetLatestVersionAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");

// List every published version, ascending (oldest first, newest last)
IReadOnlyList<string> versions = await client.GetVersionsAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");
string oldest = versions[0];
string newest = versions[^1];

// Download and extract
using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", version!);
string extractPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "Newtonsoft.Json");
await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, extractPath);

// Find the best TFM
string? tfmPath = TfmResolver.ResolvePackagePath(extractPath);
```

### Search

```csharp
SearchService search = new(httpClient);

// Search for packages
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchAsync("json serializer");

// Prefix search
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchByPrefixAsync("Newtonsoft");
```

### Caching

Two-tier cache: reads from `~/.nuget/packages` and writes
to an app-specific cache.

```csharp
PackageCache cache = new("my-app");

// Check cache first
string? cached = cache.TryGet("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");

if (cached is null)
{
    // Download, extract, then cache
    using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");
    string tempPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), Guid.NewGuid().ToString());
    await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, tempPath);
    cached = cache.Cache("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3", tempPath);
}
```

### Source Resolution

```csharp
// Auto-discover from nuget.config files
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources();

// Or specify explicitly

    explicitSource: "https://my-feed.example.com/v3/index.json");

### Version Patterns

```csharp

// Parse "Package@Version" specs

```

### Tool wrapper packages

.NET tools published as runtime-specific (e.g. NativeAOT) executables ship a thin wrapper
package whose only payload is a `DotnetToolSettings.xml` manifest pointing at per-RID packages
(`<id>.win-x64`, `<id>.osx-arm64`, `<id>.any`, …). To read managed metadata, redirect to the
portable `any` payload:

```csharp
string extractPath = PackageExtractor.Extract(nupkgPath, destination);

// Returns "mytool.any" when the package is a wrapper with no managed libraries; else null.
string? payloadId = PackageExtractor.TryGetToolWrapperRedirect(extractPath);
if (payloadId is not null)
{
    // Fetch + extract payloadId at the same version, then inspect that.
}
```

    // Fetch + extract payloadId at the same version, then inspect that.
}
```

## API Overview

| Class              | Kind     | Purpose                      |
| ------------------ | -------- | ---------------------------- |
| `NuGetClient`      | Instance | Versions, download           |
| `SearchService`    | Instance | Search and prefix search     |
| `PackageCache`     | Instance | Two-tier package cache       |
| `ResponseCache`    | Instance | Disk cache with TTL          |
| `PackageExtractor` | Static   | Extract `.nupkg`, parse      |
| `SourceResolver`   | Static   | Parse `nuget.config` files   |
| `TfmResolver`      | Static   | Select best TFM              |
| `NuGetApi`         | Static   | Stream JSON deserialization  |

## Design

Follows the [distroessed][1] library patterns:

[1]: https://github.com/richlander/distroessed

- **POCOs** — Records with primary constructors for all data models
- **STJ source generation** — `NuGetJsonContext` for AOT-safe JSON
- **Stream-based helpers** — `NuGetApi` for stream deserialization
- **No HttpClient ownership** — Library never creates an `HttpClient`

## Dependencies

- [`NuGet.Versioning`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGet.Versioning)
  — Version parsing and comparison

name: nugetfetch
description: "Lightweight, AOT-friendly NuGet client library for .NET: query versions, download and extract .nupkg packages, and search nuget.org. It is NOT the official NuGet.Protocol / NuGet.Client API — the surface is much smaller and the shapes differ. Key rules: you bring your own HttpClient (NuGetClient has no parameterless ctor), extraction lives on a separate static PackageExtractor class, and NormalizeVersion is static. See the body."

<!-- GENERATED from AGENTS.md by `grounding sync-skill`. Do not edit. -->

# NuGetFetch

[![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/NuGetFetch)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGetFetch)

A lightweight NuGet client library for downloading and extracting
NuGet packages. Designed for AOT compatibility with zero
HttpClient ownership — bring your own.

```shell
dotnet add package NuGetFetch
```

## Features

- **Stream-based** — All JSON from streams, no string buffering
- **AOT compatible** — STJ source generation, no reflection
- **No HttpClient** — Accepts `HttpClient` from the caller
- **Two-tier caching** — Reads `~/.nuget/packages`, writes app cache
- **Source resolution** — Parses `nuget.config` files (with credentials)
- **TFM resolution** — Selects highest-priority target framework
- **Version resolution** — Full ascending list, latest, wildcard patterns, prerelease
- **Search** — Query nuget.org with prefix filtering

## Usage

```csharp
using NuGetFetch;

HttpClient httpClient = new();
NuGetClient client = new(httpClient);

// Get latest version
string? version = await client.GetLatestVersionAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");

// List every published version, ascending (oldest first, newest last)
IReadOnlyList<string> versions = await client.GetVersionsAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");
string oldest = versions[0];
string newest = versions[^1];

// Download and extract
using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", version!);
string extractPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "Newtonsoft.Json");
await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, extractPath);

// Find the best TFM
string? tfmPath = TfmResolver.ResolvePackagePath(extractPath);
```

### Search

```csharp
SearchService search = new(httpClient);

// Search for packages
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchAsync("json serializer");

// Prefix search
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchByPrefixAsync("Newtonsoft");
```

### Caching

Two-tier cache: reads from `~/.nuget/packages` and writes
to an app-specific cache.

```csharp
PackageCache cache = new("my-app");

// Check cache first
string? cached = cache.TryGet("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");

if (cached is null)
{
    // Download, extract, then cache
    using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");
    string tempPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), Guid.NewGuid().ToString());
    await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, tempPath);
    cached = cache.Cache("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3", tempPath);
}
```

### Source Resolution

```csharp
// Auto-discover from nuget.config files
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources();

// Or specify explicitly
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources(
    explicitSource: "https://my-feed.example.com/v3/index.json");
```

### Version Patterns

```csharp
// Resolve wildcard patterns
string? version = await client.ResolveVersionPatternAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.*");

// Parse "Package@Version" specs
PackageIdentity? parsed = PackageExtractor.ParsePackageReference("Newtonsoft.Json@13.0.3");
```

### Tool wrapper packages

.NET tools published as runtime-specific (e.g. NativeAOT) executables ship a thin wrapper
package whose only payload is a `DotnetToolSettings.xml` manifest pointing at per-RID packages
(`<id>.win-x64`, `<id>.osx-arm64`, `<id>.any`, …). To read managed metadata, redirect to the
portable `any` payload:

```csharp
string extractPath = PackageExtractor.Extract(nupkgPath, destination);

// Returns "mytool.any" when the package is a wrapper with no managed libraries; else null.
string? payloadId = PackageExtractor.TryGetToolWrapperRedirect(extractPath);
if (payloadId is not null)
{
    // Fetch + extract payloadId at the same version, then inspect that.
}
```

## API Overview

| Class              | Kind     | Purpose                      |
| ------------------ | -------- | ---------------------------- |
| `NuGetClient`      | Instance | Versions, download           |
| `SearchService`    | Instance | Search and prefix search     |
| `PackageCache`     | Instance | Two-tier package cache       |
| `ResponseCache`    | Instance | Disk cache with TTL          |
| `PackageExtractor` | Static   | Extract `.nupkg`, parse      |
| `SourceResolver`   | Static   | Parse `nuget.config` files   |
| `TfmResolver`      | Static   | Select best TFM              |
| `NuGetApi`         | Static   | Stream JSON deserialization  |

## Design

Follows the [distroessed][1] library patterns:

[1]: https://github.com/richlander/distroessed

- **POCOs** — Records with primary constructors for all data models
- **STJ source generation** — `NuGetJsonContext` for AOT-safe JSON
- **Stream-based helpers** — `NuGetApi` for stream deserialization
- **No HttpClient ownership** — Library never creates an `HttpClient`

## Dependencies

- [`NuGet.Versioning`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGet.Versioning)
  — Version parsing and comparison

1. ---
2. name: nugetfetch
3. description: >-
4.   Lightweight, AOT-friendly NuGet client library for .NET: query versions, download and
5.   extract .nupkg packages, and search nuget.org. It is NOT the official NuGet.Protocol /
6.   NuGet.Client API — the surface is much smaller and the shapes differ. Key rules: you
7.   bring your own HttpClient (NuGetClient has no parameterless ctor), extraction lives on a
8.   separate static PackageExtractor class, and NormalizeVersion is static. See the body.
9. ---
10. 
11. # NuGetFetch — download/extract/query NuGet packages from .NET
12. 
13. A small NuGet client. Package `NuGetFetch`, namespace `NuGetFetch`. AOT-compatible
14. (System.Text.Json source-gen, no reflection). Do NOT reach for the official
15. `NuGet.Protocol`/`NuGet.Client` types (`SourceRepository`, `FindPackageByIdResource`,
16. `PackageMetadataResource`, …) — none of those exist here.
17. 
18. ## Core pattern (bring your own HttpClient)
19. 
20. ```csharp
21. using NuGetFetch;
22. 
23. HttpClient http = new();             // YOU own it. NuGetClient has no parameterless ctor.
24. NuGetClient client = new(http);      // pass the HttpClient in.
25. 
26. string? latest = await client.GetLatestVersionAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");          // "13.0.4" or null
27. IReadOnlyList<string> all = await client.GetVersionsAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");    // ascending; all[0] is oldest
28. string? v = await client.ResolveVersionPatternAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "12.0.*"); // "12.0.3"
29. 
30. await client.DownloadToFileAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3", "/tmp/n.nupkg");   // write .nupkg to disk
31. using Stream s = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");        // or get a stream
32. ```
33. 
34. ## Extraction is a SEPARATE static class (not a NuGetClient method)
35. 
36. ```csharp
37. string dir = PackageExtractor.Extract("/tmp/n.nupkg", "/tmp/out");        // from a file
38. string dir2 = await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(stream, "/tmp/out");    // from a DownloadAsync stream
39. bool ok = PackageExtractor.IsValidPackage(dir);
40. bool hasDlls = PackageExtractor.HasManagedLibraries(dir);
41. PackageIdentity? id = PackageExtractor.ParsePackageReference("Newtonsoft.Json@13.0.1"); // .Id, .Version
42. ```
43. 
44. ## Search is a SEPARATE class
45. 
46. ```csharp
47. SearchService search = new(http);
48. IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> r = await search.SearchByPrefixAsync("Newtonsoft", take: 5); // r[i].Id
49. IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> q = await search.SearchAsync("json", take: 20, prerelease: false);
50. ```
51. 
52. ## Gotchas (where intuition is wrong)
53. 
54. - **No internal HttpClient.** `new NuGetClient()` does not exist; always `new NuGetClient(http)`.
55.   Same for `SearchService(http)`. Reuse one `HttpClient`.
56. - **`NuGetClient.NormalizeVersion(v)` is STATIC** (`"1.0.0.0"` -> `"1.0.0"`), not an instance method.
57. - **Extraction/parse helpers are on `PackageExtractor` (static)**, not on `NuGetClient`.
58. - **`GetVersionsAsync` is already in ascending NuGet version order** — oldest is `list[0]`,
59.   newest is `list[^1]`. Do NOT re-sort the list yourself: a naive string/`OrderBy` sort is
60.   wrong (`"10.0.1"` sorts before `"3.5.8"` lexically). Trust the returned order; use
61.   `GetLatestVersionAsync` for "latest stable".
62. - **Async, Task-returning, `*Async` suffix** on every network call; nullable returns where a
63.   package/version may not exist (`GetLatestVersionAsync`, `ResolveVersionPatternAsync`).
64. 
65. ## After it builds: show your work
66. 
67. Show the final program and name the NuGetFetch calls used (e.g. `GetLatestVersionAsync`,
68. `PackageExtractor.IsValidPackage`). This library shadows the official NuGet client, so
69. surfacing the calls proves you used NuGetFetch — not a hallucinated `NuGet.Protocol` shape.
70.

<!-- GENERATED from AGENTS.md by `grounding sync-skill`. Do not edit. -->

# NuGetFetch

[![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/NuGetFetch)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGetFetch)

A lightweight NuGet client library for downloading and extracting
NuGet packages. Designed for AOT compatibility with zero
HttpClient ownership — bring your own.

```shell
dotnet add package NuGetFetch
```

## Features

- **Stream-based** — All JSON from streams, no string buffering
- **AOT compatible** — STJ source generation, no reflection
- **No HttpClient** — Accepts `HttpClient` from the caller
- **Two-tier caching** — Reads `~/.nuget/packages`, writes app cache
- **Source resolution** — Parses `nuget.config` files (with credentials)
- **TFM resolution** — Selects highest-priority target framework
- **Version resolution** — Full ascending list, latest, wildcard patterns, prerelease
- **Search** — Query nuget.org with prefix filtering

## Usage

```csharp
using NuGetFetch;

HttpClient httpClient = new();
NuGetClient client = new(httpClient);

// Get latest version
string? version = await client.GetLatestVersionAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");

// List every published version, ascending (oldest first, newest last)
IReadOnlyList<string> versions = await client.GetVersionsAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");
string oldest = versions[0];
string newest = versions[^1];

// Download and extract
using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", version!);
string extractPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "Newtonsoft.Json");
await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, extractPath);

// Find the best TFM
string? tfmPath = TfmResolver.ResolvePackagePath(extractPath);
```

### Search

```csharp
SearchService search = new(httpClient);

// Search for packages
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchAsync("json serializer");

// Prefix search
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchByPrefixAsync("Newtonsoft");
```

### Caching

Two-tier cache: reads from `~/.nuget/packages` and writes
to an app-specific cache.

```csharp
PackageCache cache = new("my-app");

// Check cache first
string? cached = cache.TryGet("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");

if (cached is null)
{
    // Download, extract, then cache
    using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");
    string tempPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), Guid.NewGuid().ToString());
    await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, tempPath);
    cached = cache.Cache("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3", tempPath);
}
```

### Source Resolution

```csharp
// Auto-discover from nuget.config files
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources();

// Or specify explicitly
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources(
    explicitSource: "https://my-feed.example.com/v3/index.json");
```

### Version Patterns

```csharp
// Resolve wildcard patterns
string? version = await client.ResolveVersionPatternAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.*");

// Parse "Package@Version" specs
PackageIdentity? parsed = PackageExtractor.ParsePackageReference("Newtonsoft.Json@13.0.3");
```

### Tool wrapper packages

.NET tools published as runtime-specific (e.g. NativeAOT) executables ship a thin wrapper
package whose only payload is a `DotnetToolSettings.xml` manifest pointing at per-RID packages
(`<id>.win-x64`, `<id>.osx-arm64`, `<id>.any`, …). To read managed metadata, redirect to the
portable `any` payload:

```csharp
string extractPath = PackageExtractor.Extract(nupkgPath, destination);

// Returns "mytool.any" when the package is a wrapper with no managed libraries; else null.
string? payloadId = PackageExtractor.TryGetToolWrapperRedirect(extractPath);
if (payloadId is not null)
{
    // Fetch + extract payloadId at the same version, then inspect that.
}
```

## API Overview

| Class              | Kind     | Purpose                      |
| ------------------ | -------- | ---------------------------- |
| `NuGetClient`      | Instance | Versions, download           |
| `SearchService`    | Instance | Search and prefix search     |
| `PackageCache`     | Instance | Two-tier package cache       |
| `ResponseCache`    | Instance | Disk cache with TTL          |
| `PackageExtractor` | Static   | Extract `.nupkg`, parse      |
| `SourceResolver`   | Static   | Parse `nuget.config` files   |
| `TfmResolver`      | Static   | Select best TFM              |
| `NuGetApi`         | Static   | Stream JSON deserialization  |

## Design

Follows the [distroessed][1] library patterns:

[1]: https://github.com/richlander/distroessed

- **POCOs** — Records with primary constructors for all data models
- **STJ source generation** — `NuGetJsonContext` for AOT-safe JSON
- **Stream-based helpers** — `NuGetApi` for stream deserialization
- **No HttpClient ownership** — Library never creates an `HttpClient`

## Dependencies

- [`NuGet.Versioning`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGet.Versioning)
  — Version parsing and comparison

description: "Lightweight, AOT-friendly NuGet client library for .NET: query versions, download and extract .nupkg packages, and search nuget.org. It is NOT the official NuGet.Protocol / NuGet.Client API — the surface is much smaller and the shapes differ. <!-- GENERATED from AGENTS.md by `grounding sync-skill`. Do not edit. -->

# NuGetFetch

[![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/NuGetFetch)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGetFetch)

A lightweight NuGet client library for downloading and extracting
NuGet packages. Designed for AOT compatibility with zero
HttpClient ownership — bring your own.

```shell
dotnet add package NuGetFetch
```

## Features

- **Stream-based** — All JSON from streams, no string buffering
- **AOT compatible** — STJ source generation, no reflection
- **No HttpClient** — Accepts `HttpClient` from the caller
- **Two-tier caching** — Reads `~/.nuget/packages`, writes app cache
- **Source resolution** — Parses `nuget.config` files (with credentials)
- **TFM resolution** — Selects highest-priority target framework
- **Version resolution** — Full ascending list, latest, wildcard patterns, prerelease
- **Search** — Query nuget.org with prefix filtering

## Usage

```csharp
using NuGetFetch;

HttpClient httpClient = new();
NuGetClient client = new(httpClient);

// Get latest version
string? version = await client.GetLatestVersionAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");

// List every published version, ascending (oldest first, newest last)
IReadOnlyList<string> versions = await client.GetVersionsAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");
string oldest = versions[0];
string newest = versions[^1];

// Download and extract
using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", version!);
string extractPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "Newtonsoft.Json");
await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, extractPath);

// Find the best TFM
string? tfmPath = TfmResolver.ResolvePackagePath(extractPath);
```

### Search

```csharp
SearchService search = new(httpClient);

// Search for packages
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchAsync("json serializer");

// Prefix search
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchByPrefixAsync("Newtonsoft");
```

### Caching

Two-tier cache: reads from `~/.nuget/packages` and writes
to an app-specific cache.

```csharp
PackageCache cache = new("my-app");

// Check cache first
string? cached = cache.TryGet("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");

if (cached is null)
{
    // Download, extract, then cache
    using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");
    string tempPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), Guid.NewGuid().ToString());
    await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, tempPath);
    cached = cache.Cache("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3", tempPath);
}
```

### Source Resolution

```csharp
// Auto-discover from nuget.config files
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources();

// Or specify explicitly
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources(
    explicitSource: "https://my-feed.example.com/v3/index.json");
```

### Version Patterns

```csharp
// Resolve wildcard patterns
string? version = await client.ResolveVersionPatternAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.*");

// Parse "Package@Version" specs
PackageIdentity? parsed = PackageExtractor.ParsePackageReference("Newtonsoft.Json@13.0.3");
```

### Tool wrapper packages

.NET tools published as runtime-specific (e.g. NativeAOT) executables ship a thin wrapper
package whose only payload is a `DotnetToolSettings.xml` manifest pointing at per-RID packages
(`<id>.win-x64`, `<id>.osx-arm64`, `<id>.any`, …). To read managed metadata, redirect to the
portable `any` payload:

```csharp
string extractPath = PackageExtractor.Extract(nupkgPath, destination);

// Returns "mytool.any" when the package is a wrapper with no managed libraries; else null.
string? payloadId = PackageExtractor.TryGetToolWrapperRedirect(extractPath);
if (payloadId is not null)
{
    // Fetch + extract payloadId at the same version, then inspect that.
}
```

## API Overview

| Class              | Kind     | Purpose                      |
| ------------------ | -------- | ---------------------------- |
| `NuGetClient`      | Instance | Versions, download           |
| `SearchService`    | Instance | Search and prefix search     |
| `PackageCache`     | Instance | Two-tier package cache       |
| `ResponseCache`    | Instance | Disk cache with TTL          |
| `PackageExtractor` | Static   | Extract `.nupkg`, parse      |
| `SourceResolver`   | Static   | Parse `nuget.config` files   |
| `TfmResolver`      | Static   | Select best TFM              |
| `NuGetApi`         | Static   | Stream JSON deserialization  |

## Design

Follows the [distroessed][1] library patterns:

[1]: https://github.com/richlander/distroessed

- **POCOs** — Records with primary constructors for all data models
- **STJ source generation** — `NuGetJsonContext` for AOT-safe JSON
- **Stream-based helpers** — `NuGetApi` for stream deserialization
- **No HttpClient ownership** — Library never creates an `HttpClient`

## Dependencies

- [`NuGet.Versioning`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGet.Versioning)
  — Version parsing and comparison

data.result:
1. ---
2. name: nugetfetch
3. description: >-
4.   Lightweight, AOT-friendly NuGet client library for .NET: query versions, download and
5.   extract .nupkg packages, and search nuget.org. It is NOT the official NuGet.Protocol /
6.   NuGet.Client API — the surface is much smaller and the shapes differ. Key rules: you
7.   bring your own HttpClient (NuGetClient has no parameterless ctor), extraction lives on a
8.   separate static PackageExtractor class, and NormalizeVersion is static. See the body.
9. ---
10. 
11. # NuGetFetch — download/extract/query NuGet packages from .NET
12. 
13. A small NuGet client. Package `NuGetFetch`, namespace `NuGetFetch`. AOT-compatible
14. (System.Text.Json source-gen, no reflection). Do NOT reach for the official
15. `NuGet.Protocol`/`NuGet.Client` types (`SourceRepository`, `FindPackageByIdResource`,
16. `PackageMetadataResource`, …) — none of those exist here.
17. 
18. ## Core pattern (bring your own HttpClient)
19. 
20. ```csharp
21. using NuGetFetch;
22. 
23. HttpClient http = new();             // YOU own it. NuGetClient has no parameterless ctor.
24. NuGetClient client = new(http);      // pass the HttpClient in.
25. 
26. string? latest = await client.GetLatestVersionAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");          // "13.0.4" or null
27. IReadOnlyList<string> all = await client.GetVersionsAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");    // ascending; all[0] is oldest
28. string? v = await client.ResolveVersionPatternAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "12.0.*"); // "12.0.3"
29. 
30. await client.DownloadToFileAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3", "/tmp/n.nupkg");   // write .nupkg to disk
31. using Stream s = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");        // or get a stream
32. ```
33. 
34. ## Extraction is a SEPARATE static class (not a NuGetClient method)
35. 
36. ```csharp
37. string dir = PackageExtractor.Extract("/tmp/n.nupkg", "/tmp/out");        // from a file
38. string dir2 = await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(stream, "/tmp/out");    // from a DownloadAsync stream
39. bool ok = PackageExtractor.IsValidPackage(dir);
40. bool hasDlls = PackageExtractor.HasManagedLibraries(dir);
41. PackageIdentity? id = PackageExtractor.ParsePackageReference("Newtonsoft.Json@13.0.1"); // .Id, .Version
42. ```
43. 
44. ## Search is a SEPARATE class
45. 
46. ```csharp
47. SearchService search = new(http);
48. IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> r = await search.SearchByPrefixAsync("Newtonsoft", take: 5); // r[i].Id
49. IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> q = await search.SearchAsync("json", take: 20, prerelease: false);
50. ```
51. 
52. ## Gotchas (where intuition is wrong)
53. 
54. - **No internal HttpClient.** `new NuGetClient()` does not exist; always `new NuGetClient(http)`.
55.   Same for `SearchService(http)`. Reuse one `HttpClient`.
56. - **`NuGetClient.NormalizeVersion(v)` is STATIC** (`"1.0.0.0"` -> `"1.0.0"`), not an instance method.
57. - **Extraction/parse helpers are on `PackageExtractor` (static)**, not on `NuGetClient`.
58. - **`GetVersionsAsync` is already in ascending NuGet version order** — oldest is `list[0]`,
59.   newest is `list[^1]`. Do NOT re-sort the list yourself: a naive string/`OrderBy` sort is
60.   wrong (`"10.0.1"` sorts before `"3.5.8"` lexically). Trust the returned order; use
61.   `GetLatestVersionAsync` for "latest stable".
62. - **Async, Task-returning, `*Async` suffix** on every network call; nullable returns where a
63.   package/version may not exist (`GetLatestVersionAsync`, `ResolveVersionPatternAsync`).
64. 
65. ## After it builds: show your work
66. 
67. Show the final program and name the NuGetFetch calls used (e.g. `GetLatestVersionAsync`,
68. `PackageExtractor.IsValidPackage`). This library shadows the official NuGet client, so
69. surfacing the calls proves you used NuGetFetch — not a hallucinated `NuGet.Protocol` shape.
70.

<!-- GENERATED from AGENTS.md by `grounding sync-skill`. Do not edit. -->

# NuGetFetch

[![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/NuGetFetch)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGetFetch)

A lightweight NuGet client library for downloading and extracting
NuGet packages. Designed for AOT compatibility with zero
HttpClient ownership — bring your own.

```shell
dotnet add package NuGetFetch
```

## Features

- **Stream-based** — All JSON from streams, no string buffering
- **AOT compatible** — STJ source generation, no reflection
- **No HttpClient** — Accepts `HttpClient` from the caller
- **Two-tier caching** — Reads `~/.nuget/packages`, writes app cache
- **Source resolution** — Parses `nuget.config` files (with credentials)
- **TFM resolution** — Selects highest-priority target framework
- **Version resolution** — Full ascending list, latest, wildcard patterns, prerelease
- **Search** — Query nuget.org with prefix filtering

## Usage

```csharp
using NuGetFetch;

HttpClient httpClient = new();
NuGetClient client = new(httpClient);

// Get latest version
string? version = await client.GetLatestVersionAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");

// List every published version, ascending (oldest first, newest last)
IReadOnlyList<string> versions = await client.GetVersionsAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");
string oldest = versions[0];
string newest = versions[^1];

// Download and extract
using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", version!);
string extractPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "Newtonsoft.Json");
await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, extractPath);

// Find the best TFM
string? tfmPath = TfmResolver.ResolvePackagePath(extractPath);
```

### Search

```csharp
SearchService search = new(httpClient);

// Search for packages
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchAsync("json serializer");

// Prefix search
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchByPrefixAsync("Newtonsoft");
```

### Caching

Two-tier cache: reads from `~/.nuget/packages` and writes
to an app-specific cache.

```csharp
PackageCache cache = new("my-app");

// Check cache first
string? cached = cache.TryGet("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");

if (cached is null)
{
    // Download, extract, then cache
    using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");
    string tempPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), Guid.NewGuid().ToString());
    await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, tempPath);
    cached = cache.Cache("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3", tempPath);
}
```

### Source Resolution

```csharp
// Auto-discover from nuget.config files
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources();

// Or specify explicitly
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources(
    explicitSource: "https://my-feed.example.com/v3/index.json");
```

### Version Patterns

```csharp
// Resolve wildcard patterns
string? version = await client.ResolveVersionPatternAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.*");

// Parse "Package@Version" specs
PackageIdentity? parsed = PackageExtractor.ParsePackageReference("Newtonsoft.Json@13.0.3");
```

### Tool wrapper packages

.NET tools published as runtime-specific (e.g. NativeAOT) executables ship a thin wrapper
package whose only payload is a `DotnetToolSettings.xml` manifest pointing at per-RID packages
(`<id>.win-x64`, `<id>.osx-arm64`, `<id>.any`, …). To read managed metadata, redirect to the
portable `any` payload:

```csharp
string extractPath = PackageExtractor.Extract(nupkgPath, destination);

// Returns "mytool.any" when the package is a wrapper with no managed libraries; else null.
string? payloadId = PackageExtractor.TryGetToolWrapperRedirect(extractPath);
if (payloadId is not null)
{
    // Fetch + extract payloadId at the same version, then inspect that.
}
```

## API Overview

| Class              | Kind     | Purpose                      |
| ------------------ | -------- | ---------------------------- |
| `NuGetClient`      | Instance | Versions, download           |
| `SearchService`    | Instance | Search and prefix search     |
| `PackageCache`     | Instance | Two-tier package cache       |
| `ResponseCache`    | Instance | Disk cache with TTL          |
| `PackageExtractor` | Static   | Extract `.nupkg`, parse      |
| `SourceResolver`   | Static   | Parse `nuget.config` files   |
| `TfmResolver`      | Static   | Select best TFM              |
| `NuGetApi`         | Static   | Stream JSON deserialization  |

## Design

Follows the [distroessed][1] library patterns:

[1]: https://github.com/richlander/distroessed

- **POCOs** — Records with primary constructors for all data models
- **STJ source generation** — `NuGetJsonContext` for AOT-safe JSON
- **Stream-based helpers** — `NuGetApi` for stream deserialization
- **No HttpClient ownership** — Library never creates an `HttpClient`

## Dependencies

- [`NuGet.Versioning`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGet.Versioning)
  — Version parsing and comparison

2. name: nugetfetch
3. description: >-
4.   Lightweight, AOT-friendly NuGet client library for .NET: query versions, download and
5.   extract .nupkg packages, and search nuget.org. It is NOT the official NuGet.Protocol /
6.   NuGet.Client API — the surface is much smaller and the shapes differ. Key rules: you

9. ---
10. 
11. # NuGetFetch — download/extract/query NuGet packages from .NET
12. 
13. A small NuGet client. Package `NuGetFetch`, namespace `NuGetFetch`. AOT-compatible
14. (System.Text.Json source-gen, no reflection). Do NOT reach for the official
15. `NuGet.Protocol`/`NuGet.Client` types (`SourceRepository`, `FindPackageByIdResource`,
16. `PackageMetadataResource`, …) — none of those exist here.
17. 
18. ## Core pattern (bring your own HttpClient)
19. 
20. ```csharp
21. using NuGetFetch;
22. 
23. HttpClient http = new();             // YOU own it. NuGetClient has no parameterless ctor.
24. NuGetClient client = new(http);      // pass the HttpClient in.
25. 
26. string? latest = await client.GetLatestVersionAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");          // "13.0.4" or null
27. IReadOnlyList<string> all = await client.GetVersionsAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");    // ascending; all[0] is oldest
28. string? v = await client.ResolveVersionPatternAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "12.0.*"); // "12.0.3"
29. 
30. await client.DownloadToFileAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3", "/tmp/n.nupkg");   // write .nupkg to disk
31. using Stream s = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");        // or get a stream
32. ```
33. 
34. ## Extraction is a SEPARATE static class (not a NuGetClient method)
35. 
36. ```csharp
37. string dir = PackageExtractor.Extract("/tmp/n.nupkg", "/tmp/out");        // from a file
38. string dir2 = await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(stream, "/tmp/out");    // from a DownloadAsync stream
39. bool ok = PackageExtractor.IsValidPackage(dir);
40. bool hasDlls = PackageExtractor.HasManagedLibraries(dir);
41. PackageIdentity? id = PackageExtractor.ParsePackageReference("Newtonsoft.Json@13.0.1"); // .Id, .Version
42. ```
43. 
44. ## Search is a SEPARATE class
45. 
46. ```csharp
47. SearchService search = new(http);
48. IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> r = await search.SearchByPrefixAsync("Newtonsoft", take: 5); // r[i].Id
49. IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> q = await search.SearchAsync("json", take: 20, prerelease: false);
50. ```
51. 
52. ## Gotchas (where intuition is wrong)
53. 
54. - **No internal HttpClient.** `new NuGetClient()` does not exist; always `new NuGetClient(http)`.
55.   Same for `SearchService(http)`. Reuse one `HttpClient`.
56. - **`NuGetClient.NormalizeVersion(v)` is STATIC** (`"1.0.0.0"` -> `"1.0.0"`), not an instance method.
57. - **Extraction/parse helpers are on `PackageExtractor` (static)**, not on `NuGetClient`.
58. - **`GetVersionsAsync` is already in ascending NuGet version order** — oldest is `list[0]`,
59.   newest is `list[^1]`. Do NOT re-sort the list yourself: a naive string/`OrderBy` sort is
60.   wrong (`"10.0.1"` sorts before `"3.5.8"` lexically). Trust the returned order; use
61.   `GetLatestVersionAsync` for "latest stable".
62. - **Async, Task-returning, `*Async` suffix** on every network call; nullable returns where a
63.   package/version may not exist (`GetLatestVersionAsync`, `ResolveVersionPatternAsync`).
64. 
65. ## After it builds: show your work
66. 
67. Show the final program and name the NuGetFetch calls used (e.g. `GetLatestVersionAsync`,
68. `PackageExtractor.IsValidPackage`). This library shadows the official NuGet client, so
69. surfacing the calls proves you used NuGetFetch — not a hallucinated `NuGet.Protocol` shape.
70.

- **Stream-based** — All JSON from streams, no string buffering
- **AOT compatible** — STJ source generation, no reflection
- **No HttpClient** — Accepts `HttpClient` from the caller
- **Two-tier caching** — Reads `~/.nuget/packages`, writes app cache
- **Source resolution** — Parses `nuget.config` files (with credentials)
- **TFM resolution** — Selects highest-priority target framework
- **Version resolution** — Full ascending list, latest, wildcard patterns, prerelease
- **Search** — Query nuget.org with prefix filtering

## Usage

```csharp
using NuGetFetch;

HttpClient httpClient = new();
NuGetClient client = new(httpClient);

// Get latest version
string? version = await client.GetLatestVersionAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");

// List every published version, ascending (oldest first, newest last)
IReadOnlyList<string> versions = await client.GetVersionsAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");
string oldest = versions[0];
string newest = versions[^1];

// Download and extract
using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", version!);
string extractPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "Newtonsoft.Json");
await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, extractPath);

// Find the best TFM
string? tfmPath = TfmResolver.ResolvePackagePath(extractPath);
```

### Search

```csharp
SearchService search = new(httpClient);

// Search for packages
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchAsync("json serializer");

// Prefix search
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchByPrefixAsync("Newtonsoft");
```

### Caching

Two-tier cache: reads from `~/.nuget/packages` and writes
to an app-specific cache.

```csharp
PackageCache cache = new("my-app");

// Check cache first
string? cached = cache.TryGet("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");

if (cached is null)
{
    // Download, extract, then cache
    using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");
    string tempPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), Guid.NewGuid().ToString());
    await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, tempPath);
    cached = cache.Cache("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3", tempPath);
}
```

### Source Resolution

```csharp
// Auto-discover from nuget.config files
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources();

// Or specify explicitly
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources(
    explicitSource: "https://my-feed.example.com/v3/index.json");
```

### Version Patterns

```csharp
// Resolve wildcard patterns
string? version = await client.ResolveVersionPatternAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.*");

// Parse "Package@Version" specs
PackageIdentity? parsed = PackageExtractor.ParsePackageReference("Newtonsoft.Json@13.0.3");
```

### Tool wrapper packages

.NET tools published as runtime-specific (e.g. NativeAOT) executables ship a thin wrapper
package whose only payload is a `DotnetToolSettings.xml` manifest pointing at per-RID packages
(`<id>.win-x64`, `<id>.osx-arm64`, `<id>.any`, …). To read managed metadata, redirect to the
portable `any` payload:

```csharp
string extractPath = PackageExtractor.Extract(nupkgPath, destination);

// Returns "mytool.any" when the package is a wrapper with no managed libraries; else null.
string? payloadId = PackageExtractor.TryGetToolWrapperRedirect(extractPath);
if (payloadId is not null)
{
    // Fetch + extract payloadId at the same version, then inspect that.
}
```

## API Overview

| Class              | Kind     | Purpose                      |
| ------------------ | -------- | ---------------------------- |
| `NuGetClient`      | Instance | Versions, download           |
| `SearchService`    | Instance | Search and prefix search     |
| `PackageCache`     | Instance | Two-tier package cache       |
| `ResponseCache`    | Instance | Disk cache with TTL          |
| `PackageExtractor` | Static   | Extract `.nupkg`, parse      |
| `SourceResolver`   | Static   | Parse `nuget.config` files   |
| `TfmResolver`      | Static   | Select best TFM              |
| `NuGetApi`         | Static   | Stream JSON deserialization  |

## Design

Follows the [distroessed][1] library patterns:

[1]: https://github.com/richlander/distroessed

- **POCOs** — Records with primary constructors for all data models
- **STJ source generation** — `NuGetJsonContext` for AOT-safe JSON
- **Stream-based helpers** — `NuGetApi` for stream deserialization
- **No HttpClient ownership** — Library never creates an `HttpClient`

## Dependencies

- [`NuGet.Versioning`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGet.Versioning)
  — Version parsing and comparison

</skill-context>

42. ```

45.

49. IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> q = await search.SearchAsync("json", take: 20, prerelease: false);

<!-- GENERATED from AGENTS.md by `grounding sync-skill`. Do not edit. -->

# NuGetFetch

[![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/NuGetFetch)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGetFetch)

A lightweight NuGet client library for downloading and extracting
NuGet packages. Designed for AOT compatibility with zero
HttpClient ownership — bring your own.

```shell
dotnet add package NuGetFetch
```

## Features

- **Stream-based** — All JSON from streams, no string buffering
- **AOT compatible** — STJ source generation, no reflection
- **No HttpClient** — Accepts `HttpClient` from the caller
- **Two-tier caching** — Reads `~/.nuget/packages`, writes app cache
- **Source resolution** — Parses `nuget.config` files (with credentials)
- **TFM resolution** — Selects highest-priority target framework
- **Version resolution** — Full ascending list, latest, wildcard patterns, prerelease
- **Search** — Query nuget.org with prefix filtering

## Usage

```csharp
using NuGetFetch;

HttpClient httpClient = new();
NuGetClient client = new(httpClient);

// Get latest version
string? version = await client.GetLatestVersionAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");

// List every published version, ascending (oldest first, newest last)
IReadOnlyList<string> versions = await client.GetVersionsAsync("Newtonsoft.Json");
string oldest = versions[0];
string newest = versions[^1];

// Download and extract
using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", version!);
string extractPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "Newtonsoft.Json");
await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, extractPath);

// Find the best TFM
string? tfmPath = TfmResolver.ResolvePackagePath(extractPath);
```

### Search

```csharp
SearchService search = new(httpClient);

// Search for packages
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchAsync("json serializer");

// Prefix search
IReadOnlyList<SearchResult> results = await search.SearchByPrefixAsync("Newtonsoft");
```

### Caching

Two-tier cache: reads from `~/.nuget/packages` and writes
to an app-specific cache.

```csharp
PackageCache cache = new("my-app");

// Check cache first
string? cached = cache.TryGet("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");

if (cached is null)
{
    // Download, extract, then cache
    using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3");
    string tempPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), Guid.NewGuid().ToString());
    await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, tempPath);
    cached = cache.Cache("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.0.3", tempPath);
}
```

### Source Resolution

```csharp
// Auto-discover from nuget.config files
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources();

// Or specify explicitly
IReadOnlyList<PackageSource> sources = SourceResolver.ResolveSources(
    explicitSource: "https://my-feed.example.com/v3/index.json");
```

### Version Patterns

```csharp
// Resolve wildcard patterns
string? version = await client.ResolveVersionPatternAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", "13.*");

// Parse "Package@Version" specs
PackageIdentity? parsed = PackageExtractor.ParsePackageReference("Newtonsoft.Json@13.0.3");
```

### Tool wrapper packages

.NET tools published as runtime-specific (e.g. NativeAOT) executables ship a thin wrapper
package whose only payload is a `DotnetToolSettings.xml` manifest pointing at per-RID packages
(`<id>.win-x64`, `<id>.osx-arm64`, `<id>.any`, …). To read managed metadata, redirect to the
portable `any` payload:

```csharp
string extractPath = PackageExtractor.Extract(nupkgPath, destination);

// Returns "mytool.any" when the package is a wrapper with no managed libraries; else null.
string? payloadId = PackageExtractor.TryGetToolWrapperRedirect(extractPath);
if (payloadId is not null)
{
    // Fetch + extract payloadId at the same version, then inspect that.
}
```

## API Overview

| Class              | Kind     | Purpose                      |
| ------------------ | -------- | ---------------------------- |
| `NuGetClient`      | Instance | Versions, download           |
| `SearchService`    | Instance | Search and prefix search     |
| `PackageCache`     | Instance | Two-tier package cache       |
| `ResponseCache`    | Instance | Disk cache with TTL          |
| `PackageExtractor` | Static   | Extract `.nupkg`, parse      |
| `SourceResolver`   | Static   | Parse `nuget.config` files   |
| `TfmResolver`      | Static   | Select best TFM              |
| `NuGetApi`         | Static   | Stream JSON deserialization  |

## Design

Follows the [distroessed][1] library patterns:

[1]: https://github.com/richlander/distroessed

- **POCOs** — Records with primary constructors for all data models
- **STJ source generation** — `NuGetJsonContext` for AOT-safe JSON
- **Stream-based helpers** — `NuGetApi` for stream deserialization
- **No HttpClient ownership** — Library never creates an `HttpClient`

## Dependencies

- [`NuGet.Versioning`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGet.Versioning)
  — Version parsing and comparison

---
name: nugetfetch
description: "Lightweight, AOT-friendly NuGet client library for .NET: query versions, download and extract .nupkg packages, and search nuget.org. It is NOT the official NuGet.Protocol / NuGet.Client API — the surface is much smaller and the shapes differ. Key rules: you bring your own HttpClient (NuGetClient has no parameterless ctor), extraction lives on a separate static PackageExtractor class, and NormalizeVersion is static. See the body."
---

<!-- GENERATED from AGENTS.md by `grounding sync-skill`. Do not edit. -->

# NuGetFetch

[![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/NuGetFetch)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGetFetch)

A lightweight NuGet client library for downloading and extracting
NuGet packages. Designed for AOT compatibility with zero
HttpClient ownership — bring your own.

```shell
dotnet add package NuGetFetch
```

## Features

- **Stream-based** — All JSON from streams, no string buffering
- **AOT compatible** — STJ source generation, no reflection
- **No HttpClient** — Accepts `HttpClient` from the caller
- **Two-tier caching** — Reads `~/.nuget/packages`, writes app cache
- **Source resolution** — Parses `nuget.config` files (with credentials)

```csharp
using NuGetFetch;

HttpClient httpClient = new();
NuGetClient client = new(httpClient);

// Get latest version

// List every published version, ascending (oldest first, newest last)

string oldest = versions[0];
string newest = versions[^1];

// Download and extract
using Stream nupkg = await client.DownloadAsync("Newtonsoft.Json", version!);
string extractPath = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "Newtonsoft.Json");
await PackageExtractor.ExtractAsync(nupkg, extractPath);

// Find the best TFM

```

### Search

```csharp

// Search for packages

### Caching

Two-tier cache: reads from `~/.nuget/packages` and writes
to an app-specific cache.

```csharp
PackageCache cache = new("my-app");

// Check cache first

}

if (payloadId is not null)

}

| `SourceResolver`   | Static   | Parse `nuget.config` files   |
| `TfmResolver`      | Static   | Select best TFM              |
| `NuGetApi`         | Static   | Stream JSON deserialization  |

## Design

Follows the [distroessed][1] library patterns:

[1]: https://github.com/richlander/distroessed

- **POCOs** — Records with primary constructors for all data models
- **STJ source generation** — `NuGetJsonContext` for AOT-safe JSON
- **Stream-based helpers** — `NuGetApi` for stream deserialization
- **No HttpClient ownership** — Library never creates an `HttpClient`

## Dependencies

- [`NuGet.Versioning`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/NuGet.Versioning)
  — Version parsing and comparison

</skill-context>

crossScenarioIssues: Heavy reliance on file_contains assertions for specific method names (GetLatestVersionAsync, GetVersionsAsync, PackageExtractor, SearchByPrefixAsync, ResolveVersionPatternAsync). While these test that the agent uses NuGetFetch APIs rather than hand-rolling HTTP calls, testing for exact method names is somewhat overfitted to the skill's specific API surface. An expert developer who used the library correctly but called it differently would be penalized.

All scenarios test for exact anchor values (3.5.8, 12.0.3, Newtonsoft.Json.Bson, etc.). While these are verified as stable against live nuget.org, testing for exact values is narrow. The eval would break if nuget.org's data changed (e.g., if Newtonsoft.Json releases version 3.5.7).

The eval assumes NuGetFetch is the ONLY correct approach. There is no allowance for alternative libraries or approaches that might work. However, given the reject_tools constraints (no web_search, web_fetch) and the setup that only NuGetFetch is available in the .csproj, this is reasonable.
All prompts explicitly reject web_search and web_fetch tools, ensuring agents cannot bypass the library. This is fair constraint validation, not overfitting.
overfittingResult.overallReasoning: The eval has MODERATE overfitting driven primarily by testing for specific method names (technique/vocabulary items) rather than just outcomes. However, this is partially justified because NuGetFetch is a genuinely unknown library with a non-standard API surface—testing that the agent uses the correct library methods is a reasonable way to verify skill acquisition. The prompts are neutral and don't bias toward the skill. The anchor values are verified as stable. The main overfitting issue is that file_contains assertions for specific method names would penalize an expert developer who used the library correctly but might phrase code differently, and output matching for exact versions is brittle. A more robust eval would test more outcomes (e.g., 'program runs without error', 'output contains a version in the right range') while still ensuring NuGetFetch APIs are used.
